item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of the Lord Huron song "Ghost on the Shore" for obvious reasons. All in all, this was a nicely satisfying read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this. The atmosphere is well crafted and the imagery is great. The investigation logs are also well paced and the dialogue is good enough for my liking.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is elegant in its simplicity, imo, and mercifully concise compared to some other entries. it's also beautiful and very touching. this is one of those that I wish was real. an unreserved +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am of the opinion that somethings don't really need to be overstated or grandiose to have an impact. For example, SCP-4999 is short, sweet, and extremely evocative. It's not complex, just a man who sits with you when you die. I could understand your scope complaint in that context, but not so much here. The implication is that this is where you go when you die. That this could be the afterlife. I can't really think of something much larger in scope that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is quite beautiful. It's nice to see a scip that isn't malevolent sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ack. I'm torn on this one.

+ show block
– hide block

For the first two logs, you really had me going there. It was a solid investigation, and I was intrigued to learn more and find out what the deal was, who the killer was, etc.

Then we got to the end and, well, I was underwhelmed. Not only did the big murder mystery turn out to simply be an accident, slipping down the stairs no less, but the fact that your big anomaly is a slightly grander flavor of ghosts left something to be desired. Which is a disappointment given how well you were doing up to that point.

No vote. When you hit your stride in the first two logs, you really hit it. But that ending just falls flat.

EDIT: Got a chance to check this out post update. Fixes most of the problems I mentioned above. You earned my upvote. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the effect and its significance, overall. This would be a pleasant read outside the confines of a contest.

But I also felt like there was such a logical disconnect between the anomaly and the investigation, and I spent the whole time I was reading those logs wondering just what the heck was going on. And I have to say I'm disappointed the answer was just to bring in this all-knowing figure to explain the whole thing to us. Not much mystery by the end; it's played very by the book. I think this could be improved with a longer set of logs and a more open ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For personal reasons this got me right in the feels. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilery Critique
- Close

It's… Good. The etheral image this anomaly gives really does sound great, and the story definitely does have a mystery… But it isn't really a big one. The origin of the lake ghosts is really never my largest worry, and in the end it doesen't even have a conclusion beyond it being "older than time". The other mystery of Mrs. Robinson's death also has a rather unfulfilling conclusion with her death being just an accident.

That being said… It's good. Definitely a good Bright challenge, but simply not a contender for the 5000 spot. Might not be the best aim for most writers that aren't Kaktus or not_a_seagull, but it simply isn't grandiose enough, or has a scope too small when compared to a X000.

So, +1 nevertheless. I'm still a sucker for pretty images of dancing ghosts.

item.title: Re: OwO
item.content:encoded:
Hey Nice article

How do you add URL to it

item.title: OwO
item.content:encoded:
Back at it again with another Bright's Challenge, but this time for 5k. I would like to thank my valued critters.

Choice for slots:

5204
5793
5996
5451
5999

Image
